verses.latin_vulgate: Præcepit autem Joseph dispensatori domus suæ, dicens : Imple saccos eorum frumento, quantum possunt capere : et pone pecuniam singulorum in summitate sacci.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְצַ֞ו אֶת־אֲשֶׁ֣ר עַל־בֵּיתוֹ֮ לֵאמֹר֒ מַלֵּ֞א אֶת־אַמְתְּחֹ֤ת הָֽאֲנָשִׁים֙ אֹ֔כֶל כַּאֲשֶׁ֥ר יוּכְל֖וּן שְׂאֵ֑ת וְשִׂ֥ים כֶּֽסֶף־אִ֖ישׁ בְּפִ֥י אַמְתַּחְתּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Joseph commanded the steward of his house, saying: Fill their sacks with corn, as much as they can hold: and put the money of every one in the top of his sack.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Joseph käski huoneensa haltiaa, sanoen: täytä miesten säkit jyvillä, niin paljo kuin he voivat kannattaa, ja pane itsekunkin raha säkkinsä suuhun.
verses.king_james: And he commanded the steward of his house, saying, Fill the men’s sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put every man’s money in his sack’s mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scyphum autem meum argenteum, et pretium quod dedit tritici, pone in ore sacci junioris. Factumque est ita.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־גְּבִיעִ֞י גְּבִ֣יעַ הַכֶּ֗סֶף תָּשִׂים֙ בְּפִי֙ אַמְתַּ֣חַת הַקָּטֹ֔ן וְאֵ֖ת כֶּ֣סֶף שִׁבְר֑וֹ וַיַּ֕עַשׂ כִּדְבַ֥ר יוֹסֵ֖ף אֲשֶׁ֥ר דִּבֵּֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And in the mouth of the younger's sack put my silver cup, and the price which he gave for the wheat. And it was so done.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minun maljani, se hopiamalja pane nuorimman säkin suuhun, ynnä jyväinsä hinnan kanssa. Ja hän teki niinkuin Joseph hänelle käski.
verses.king_james: And put my cup, the silver cup, in the sack’s mouth of the youngest, and his corn money. And he did according to the word that Joseph had spoken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et orto mane, dimissi sunt cum asinis suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַבֹּ֖קֶר א֑וֹר וְהָאֲנָשִׁ֣ים שֻׁלְּח֔וּ הֵ֖מָּה וַחֲמֹרֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the morning arose, they were sent away with their asses.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta aamulla päivän valjetessa, päästettiin miehet menemään aaseinensa.
verses.king_james: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent away, they and their asses.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jamque urbem exierant, et processerant paululum : tunc Joseph accersito dispensatore domus, Surge, inquit, et persequere viros : et apprehensis dicito : Quare reddidistis malum pro bono ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֠ם יָֽצְא֣וּ אֶת־הָעִיר֮ לֹ֣א הִרְחִ֒יקוּ֒ וְיוֹסֵ֤ף אָמַר֙ לַֽאֲשֶׁ֣ר עַל־בֵּית֔וֹ ק֥וּם רְדֹ֖ף אַחֲרֵ֣י הָֽאֲנָשִׁ֑ים וְהִשַּׂגְתָּם֙ וְאָמַרְתָּ֣ אֲלֵהֶ֔ם לָ֛מָּה שִׁלַּמְתֶּ֥ם רָעָ֖ה תַּ֥חַת טוֹבָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when they were now departed out of the city, and had gone forward a little way: Joseph sending for the steward of his house, said: Arise, and pursue after the men: and when thou hast overtaken them, say to them: Why have you returned evil for good?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska he olivat lähteneet kaupungista, eikä vielä kauvas joutuneet, sanoi Joseph huoneensa haltialle: nouse ja aja takaa miehiä, ja koska heidän saat takaa, niin sano heille: miksi te olette hyvän pahalla maksaneet?
verses.king_james: And when they were gone out of the city, and not yet far off, Joseph said unto his steward, Up, follow after the men; and when thou dost overtake them, say unto them, Wherefore have ye rewarded evil for good?

verses.latin_vulgate: scyphus, quem furati estis, ipse est in quo bibit dominus meus, et in quo augurari solet : pessimam rem fecistis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲל֣וֹא זֶ֗ה אֲשֶׁ֨ר יִשְׁתֶּ֤ה אֲדֹנִי֙ בּ֔וֹ וְה֕וּא נַחֵ֥שׁ יְנַחֵ֖שׁ בּ֑וֹ הֲרֵעֹתֶ֖ם אֲשֶׁ֥ר עֲשִׂיתֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: The cup which you have stolen, is that in which my lord drinketh, and in which he is wont to divine: you have done a very evil thing.
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö se ole, josta minun herrani juo? Ja josta hän kyllä hyvin taitaa arvata teistä? Te olette pahoin tehneet.
verses.king_james: Is not this it in which my lord drinketh, and whereby indeed he divineth? ye have done evil in so doing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fecit ille ut jusserat : et apprehensis per ordinem locutus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיַּשִּׂגֵ֑ם וַיְדַבֵּ֣ר אֲלֵהֶ֔ם אֶת־הַדְּבָרִ֖ים הָאֵֽלֶּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: He did as he had commanded him. And having overtaken them, he spoke to them the same words.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän käsitti heidät, puhui hän nämät sanat heille.
verses.king_james: And he overtook them, and he spake unto them these same words.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui responderunt : Quare sic loquitur dominus noster, ut servi tui tantum flagitii commiserint ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ אֵלָ֔יו לָ֚מָּה יְדַבֵּ֣ר אֲדֹנִ֔י כַּדְּבָרִ֖ים הָאֵ֑לֶּה חָלִ֙ילָה֙ לַעֲבָדֶ֔יךָ מֵעֲשׂ֖וֹת כַּדָּבָ֥ר הַזֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they answered: Why doth our lord speak so, as though thy servants had committed so heinous a fact?
verses.finnish_biblia: He vastasivat häntä: miksi minun herrani senkaltaista puhuu? Pois se, että sinun palvelias niin tekisivät.
verses.king_james: And they said unto him, Wherefore saith my lord these words? God forbid that thy servants should do according to this thing:

verses.latin_vulgate: pecuniam, quam invenimus in summitate saccorum, reportavimus ad te de terra Chanaan : et quomodo consequens est ut furati simus de domo domini tui aurum vel argentum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֣ן כֶּ֗סֶף אֲשֶׁ֤ר מָצָ֙אנוּ֙ בְּפִ֣י אַמְתְּחֹתֵ֔ינוּ הֱשִׁיבֹ֥נוּ אֵלֶ֖יךָ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ כְּנָ֑עַן וְאֵ֗יךְ נִגְנֹב֙ מִבֵּ֣ית אֲדֹנֶ֔יךָ כֶּ֖סֶף א֥וֹ זָהָֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: The money, that we found in the top of our sacks, we brought back to thee from the land of Chanaan: how then should it be that we should steal out of thy lord's house, gold or silver?
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, rahan, jonka me löysimme säkkeimme suusta, olemme me jälleen sinulle tuoneet Kanaanin maalta: kuinkasta siis me varastimme hopiaa eli kultaa sinun herras huoneesta?
verses.king_james: Behold, the money, which we found in our sacks’ mouths, we brought again unto thee out of the land of Canaan: how then should we steal out of thy lord’s house silver or gold?

verses.latin_vulgate: apud quemcumque fuerit inventum servorum tuorum quod quæris, moriatur, et nos erimus servi domini nostri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֨ר יִמָּצֵ֥א אִתּ֛וֹ מֵעֲבָדֶ֖יךָ וָמֵ֑ת וְגַם־אֲנַ֕חְנוּ נִֽהְיֶ֥ה לַֽאדֹנִ֖י לַעֲבָדִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: With whomsoever of thy servants shall be found that which thou seekest, let him die, and we will be the bondmen of my lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jonka tyköä sinun palvelioiltas se löydetään, hän kuolkaan: niin tahdomme me myös olla orjat minun herralleni.
verses.king_james: With whomsoever of thy servants it be found, both let him die, and we also will be my lord’s bondmen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixit eis : Fiat juxta vestram sententiam : apud quemcumque fuerit inventum, ipse sit servus meus, vos autem eritis innoxii.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֕אמֶר גַּם־עַתָּ֥ה כְדִבְרֵיכֶ֖ם כֶּן־ה֑וּא אֲשֶׁ֨ר יִמָּצֵ֤א אִתּוֹ֙ יִהְיֶה־לִּ֣י עָ֔בֶד וְאַתֶּ֖ם תִּהְי֥וּ נְקִיִּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to them: Let it be according to your sentence: with whomsoever it shall be found, let him be my servant, and you shall be blameless.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: olkaan nyt teidän sananne jälkeen: jonka tyköä se löydetään, hän olkaan minun orjani; mutta te olette vapaat.
verses.king_james: And he said, Now also let it be according unto your words: he with whom it is found shall be my servant; and ye shall be blameless.

verses.latin_vulgate: Itaque festinato deponentes in terram saccos, aperuerunt singuli.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְמַהֲר֗וּ וַיּוֹרִ֛דוּ אִ֥ישׁ אֶת־אַמְתַּחְתּ֖וֹ אָ֑רְצָה וַֽיִּפְתְּח֖וּ אִ֥ישׁ אַמְתַּחְתּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then they speedily took down their sacks to the ground, and every man opened his sack.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he laskivat kiiruusti itsekukin säkkinsä maahan, ja jokainen avasi säkkinsä.
verses.king_james: Then they speedily took down every man his sack to the ground, and opened every man his sack.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quos scrutatus, incipiens a majore usque ad minimum, invenit scyphum in sacco Benjamin.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְחַפֵּ֕שׂ בַּגָּד֣וֹל הֵחֵ֔ל וּבַקָּטֹ֖ן כִּלָּ֑ה וַיִּמָּצֵא֙ הַגָּבִ֔יעַ בְּאַמְתַּ֖חַת בִּנְיָמִֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Which when he had searched, beginning at the eldest, and ending at the youngest, he found the cup in Benjamin's sack.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän etsei, ruveten vanhimmasta niin nuorimpaan asti; ja hopiamalja löydettiin BenJaminin säkistä.
verses.king_james: And he searched, and began at the eldest, and left at the youngest: and the cup was found in Benjamin’s sack.

verses.latin_vulgate: At illi, scissis vestibus, oneratisque rursum asinis, reversi sunt in oppidum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיִּקְרְע֖וּ שִׂמְלֹתָ֑ם וַֽיַּעֲמֹס֙ אִ֣ישׁ עַל־חֲמֹר֔וֹ וַיָּשֻׁ֖בוּ הָעִֽירָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then they rent their garments, and loading their asses again, returned into the town.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin repäisivät vaatteensa, ja itsekukin pani kuormansa aasin päälle, ja palasivat kaupunkiin.
verses.king_james: Then they rent their clothes, and laded every man his ass, and returned to the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Primusque Judas cum fratribus ingressus est ad Joseph (necdum enim de loco abierat) omnesque ante eum pariter in terram corruerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֨א יְהוּדָ֤ה וְאֶחָיו֙ בֵּ֣יתָה יוֹסֵ֔ף וְה֖וּא עוֹדֶ֣נּוּ שָׁ֑ם וַיִּפְּל֥וּ לְפָנָ֖יו אָֽרְצָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Juda at the head of his brethren went in to Joseph (for he was not yet gone out of the place) and they all together fell down before him on the ground.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Juuda meni veljinensä Josephin huoneesen (sillä hän oli vielä silloin siellä); ja he lankesivat maahan hänen eteensä.
verses.king_james: And Judah and his brethren came to Joseph’s house; for he was yet there: and they fell before him on the ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quibus ille ait : Cur sic agere voluistis ? an ignoratis quod non sit similis mei in augurandi scientia ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לָהֶם֙ יוֹסֵ֔ף מָֽה־הַמַּעֲשֶׂ֥ה הַזֶּ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֣ר עֲשִׂיתֶ֑ם הֲל֣וֹא יְדַעְתֶּ֔ם כִּֽי־נַחֵ֧שׁ יְנַחֵ֛שׁ אִ֖ישׁ אֲשֶׁ֥ר כָּמֹֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to them: Why would you do so? know you not that there is no one like me in the science of divining.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joseph sanoi heille: mikä työ se on, kuin te tehneet olette? Ettekö te tietäneet, että senkaltainen mies kuin minä olen taitaa kyllä hyvin arvata.
verses.king_james: And Joseph said unto them, What deed is this that ye have done? wot ye not that such a man as I can certainly divine?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui Judas : Quid respondebimus, inquit, domino meo ? vel quid loquemur, aut juste poterimus obtendere ? Deus invenit iniquitatem servorum tuorum : en omnes servi sumus domini mei, et nos, et apud quem inventus est scyphus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר יְהוּדָ֗ה מַה־נֹּאמַר֙ לַֽאדֹנִ֔י מַה־נְּדַבֵּ֖ר וּמַה־נִּצְטַדָּ֑ק הָאֱלֹהִ֗ים מָצָא֙ אֶת־עֲוֺ֣ן עֲבָדֶ֔יךָ הִנֶּ֤נּוּ עֲבָדִים֙ לַֽאדֹנִ֔י גַּם־אֲנַ֕חְנוּ גַּ֛ם אֲשֶׁר־נִמְצָ֥א הַגָּבִ֖יעַ בְּיָדֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Juda said to him: What shall we answer my lord? or what shall we say, or be able justly to allege? God hath found out the iniquity of thy servants: behold, we are all bondmen to my lord, both we, and he with whom the cup was found.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Juuda sanoi: mitä me vastaamme minun herralleni? Taikka mitä me puhumme? Taikka millä me taidamme itsemme puhdistaa? Jumala on löytänyt sinun palveliais vääryyden: katso, me olemme meidän herramme orjat, sekä me, että se, jolta malja löydettiin.
verses.king_james: And Judah said, What shall we say unto my lord? what shall we speak? or how shall we clear ourselves? God hath found out the iniquity of thy servants: behold, we are my lord’s servants, both we, and he also with whom the cup is found.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Joseph : Absit a me ut sic agam : qui furatus est scyphum, ipse sit servus meus : vos autem abite liberi ad patrem vestrum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֕אמֶר חָלִ֣ילָה לִּ֔י מֵעֲשׂ֖וֹת זֹ֑את הָאִ֡ישׁ אֲשֶׁר֩ נִמְצָ֨א הַגָּבִ֜יעַ בְּיָד֗וֹ ה֚וּא יִהְיֶה־לִּ֣י עָ֔בֶד וְאַתֶּ֕ם עֲל֥וּ לְשָׁל֖וֹם אֶל־אֲבִיכֶֽם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Joseph answered: God forbid that I should do so: he that stole the cup, he shall be my bondman: and go you away free to your father.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta hän sanoi: pois se, että minä niin tekisin; sen miehen, jolta malja on löydetty, pitää oleman minun orjani; mutta menkäät te rauhassa isänne tykö.
verses.king_james: And he said, God forbid that I should do so: but the man in whose hand the cup is found, he shall be my servant; and as for you, get you up in peace unto your father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accedens autem propius Judas, confidenter ait : Oro, domine mi, loquatur servus tuus verbum in auribus tuis, et ne irascaris famulo tuo : tu es enim post Pharaonem
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגַּ֨שׁ אֵלָ֜יו יְהוּדָ֗ה וַיֹּ֘אמֶר֮ בִּ֣י אֲדֹנִי֒ יְדַבֶּר־נָ֨א עַבְדְּךָ֤ דָבָר֙ בְּאׇזְנֵ֣י אֲדֹנִ֔י וְאַל־יִ֥חַר אַפְּךָ֖ בְּעַבְדֶּ֑ךָ כִּ֥י כָמ֖וֹךָ כְּפַרְעֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Juda coming nearer, said boldly: I beseech thee, my lord, let thy servant speak a word in thy ears, and be not angry with thy servant: for after Pharao thou art.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Juuda astui hänen tykönsä, ja sanoi: ah minun herrani, suo nyt palvelias puhua yksi sana minun herrani korvissa, ja älkään julmistuko sinun vihas sinun palvelias päälle, sillä sinä olet niinkuin Pharao.
verses.king_james: Then Judah came near unto him, and said, Oh my lord, let thy servant, I pray thee, speak a word in my lord’s ears, and let not thine anger burn against thy servant: for thou art even as Pharaoh.

verses.latin_vulgate: dominus meus. Interrogasti prius servos tuos : Habetis patrem aut fratrem ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲדֹנִ֣י שָׁאַ֔ל אֶת־עֲבָדָ֖יו לֵאמֹ֑ר הֲיֵשׁ־לָכֶ֥ם אָ֖ב אוֹ־אָֽח׃
verses.douay_rheims: My lord. Thou didst ask thy servants the first time: Have you a father or a brother.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun herrani kysyi hänen palvelioiltansa, sanoen: onko teillä isää taikka veljeä?
verses.king_james: My lord asked his servants, saying, Have ye a father, or a brother?

verses.latin_vulgate: et nos respondimus tibi domino meo : Est nobis pater senex, et puer parvulus, qui in senectute illius natus est : cujus uterinus frater mortuus est : et ipsum solum habet mater sua, pater vero tenere diligit eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַנֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־אֲדֹנִ֔י יֶשׁ־לָ֙נוּ֙ אָ֣ב זָקֵ֔ן וְיֶ֥לֶד זְקֻנִ֖ים קָטָ֑ן וְאָחִ֣יו מֵ֔ת וַיִּוָּתֵ֨ר ה֧וּא לְבַדּ֛וֹ לְאִמּ֖וֹ וְאָבִ֥יו אֲהֵבֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And we answered thee, my lord: We have a father an old man, and a young boy, that was born in his old age; whose brother by the mother is dead; and he alone is left of his mother, and his father loveth him tenderly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin me vastasimme meidän herraamme: meillä on vanha isä, ja nuorukainen syntynyt hänen vanhalla ijällänsä, jonka veli on kuollut, ja hän on yksinänsä jäänyt äidistänsä, ja hänen isänsä pitää hänen rakkaana.
verses.king_james: And we said unto my lord, We have a father, an old man, and a child of his old age, a little one; and his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother, and his father loveth him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixistique servis tuis : Adducite eum ad me, et ponam oculos meos super illum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־עֲבָדֶ֔יךָ הוֹרִדֻ֖הוּ אֵלָ֑י וְאָשִׂ֥ימָה עֵינִ֖י עָלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And thou saidst to thy servants: Bring him hither to me, and I will set my eyes on him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sinä sanoit palvelioilles: tuokaat häntä tänne minun tyköni, että minä saan nähdä hänen.
verses.king_james: And thou saidst unto thy servants, Bring him down unto me, that I may set mine eyes upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Suggessimus domino meo : Non potest puer relinquere patrem suum : si enim illum dimiserit, morietur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַנֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־אֲדֹנִ֔י לֹא־יוּכַ֥ל הַנַּ֖עַר לַעֲזֹ֣ב אֶת־אָבִ֑יו וְעָזַ֥ב אֶת־אָבִ֖יו וָמֵֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: We suggested to my lord: The boy cannot leave his father: for if he leave him, he will die.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta me vastasimme minun herralleni: ei taida nuorukainen jättää isäänsä; sillä jos hän jättäis isänsä, niin hän kuolis.
verses.king_james: And we said unto my lord, The lad cannot leave his father: for if he should leave his father, his father would die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixisti servis tuis : Nisi venerit frater vester minimus vobiscum, non videbitis amplius faciem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֶל־עֲבָדֶ֔יךָ אִם־לֹ֥א יֵרֵ֛ד אֲחִיכֶ֥ם הַקָּטֹ֖ן אִתְּכֶ֑ם לֹ֥א תֹסִפ֖וּן לִרְא֥וֹת פָּנָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And thou saidst to thy servants: Except your youngest brother come with you, you shall see my face no more.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sinä sanoit palvelioilles. jollei teidän nuorin veljenne tule teidän kanssanne, niin ei teidän pidä enää minun kasvojani näkemän.
verses.king_james: And thou saidst unto thy servants, Except your youngest brother come down with you, ye shall see my face no more.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo ascendissemus ad famulum tuum patrem nostrum, narravimus ei omnia quæ locutus est dominus meus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי֙ כִּ֣י עָלִ֔ינוּ אֶֽל־עַבְדְּךָ֖ אָבִ֑י וַנַּ֨גֶּד־ל֔וֹ אֵ֖ת דִּבְרֵ֥י אֲדֹנִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Therefore when we were gone up to thy servant our father, we told him all that my lord had said.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska me menimme sinun palvelias minun isäni tykö, ja ilmoitimme hänelle minun herrani sanat,
verses.king_james: And it came to pass when we came up unto thy servant my father, we told him the words of my lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit pater noster : Revertimini, et emite nobis parum tritici.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֖אמֶר אָבִ֑ינוּ שֻׁ֖בוּ שִׁבְרוּ־לָ֥נוּ מְעַט־אֹֽכֶל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And our father said: Go again, and buy us a little wheat.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi meidän isämme: menkäät jälleen pois, ja ostakaat meille jotakin elatusta.
verses.king_james: And our father said, Go again, and buy us a little food.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui diximus : Ire non possumus : si frater noster minimus descenderit nobiscum, proficiscemur simul : alioquin illo absente, non audemus videre faciem viri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַנֹּ֕אמֶר לֹ֥א נוּכַ֖ל לָרֶ֑דֶת אִם־יֵשׁ֩ אָחִ֨ינוּ הַקָּטֹ֤ן אִתָּ֙נוּ֙ וְיָרַ֔דְנוּ כִּי־לֹ֣א נוּכַ֗ל לִרְאוֹת֙ פְּנֵ֣י הָאִ֔ישׁ וְאָחִ֥ינוּ הַקָּטֹ֖ן אֵינֶ֥נּוּ אִתָּֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And we said to him: We cannot go: if our youngest brother go down with us, we will set out together: otherwise, without him we dare not see the man's face.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta me sanoimme: emme tohdi sinne mennä; vaan jos meidän nuorin veljemme on meidän kanssamme, niin me menemme; sillä emme saa nähdä sen miehen kasvoja, jollei meidän nuorin veljemme ole meidän kanssamme.
verses.king_james: And we said, We cannot go down: if our youngest brother be with us, then will we go down: for we may not see the man’s face, except our youngest brother be with us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad quæ ille respondit : Vos scitis quod duos genuerit mihi uxor mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֛אמֶר עַבְדְּךָ֥ אָבִ֖י אֵלֵ֑ינוּ אַתֶּ֣ם יְדַעְתֶּ֔ם כִּ֥י שְׁנַ֖יִם יָֽלְדָה־לִּ֥י אִשְׁתִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Whereunto he answered: You know that my wife bore me two.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sinun palvelias minun isäni sanoi meille: te tiedätte, että minun emäntäni synnytti minulle kaksi,
verses.king_james: And thy servant my father said unto us, Ye know that my wife bare me two sons:

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressus est unus, et dixistis : Bestia devoravit eum : et hucusque non comparet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצֵ֤א הָֽאֶחָד֙ מֵֽאִתִּ֔י וָאֹמַ֕ר אַ֖ךְ טָרֹ֣ף טֹרָ֑ף וְלֹ֥א רְאִיתִ֖יו עַד־הֵֽנָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: One went out, and you said: A beast devoured him; and hitherto he appeareth not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja toinen läksi minun tyköäni, josta sanottiin, että hän on kuoliaaksi revelty, ja en ole minä häntä vielä sitte nähnyt.
verses.king_james: And the one went out from me, and I said, Surely he is torn in pieces; and I saw him not since:

verses.latin_vulgate: Si tuleritis et istum, et aliquid ei in via contigerit, deducetis canos meos cum mœrore ad inferos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּלְקַחְתֶּ֧ם גַּם־אֶת־זֶ֛ה מֵעִ֥ם פָּנַ֖י וְקָרָ֣הוּ אָס֑וֹן וְהֽוֹרַדְתֶּ֧ם אֶת־שֵׂיבָתִ֛י בְּרָעָ֖ה שְׁאֹֽלָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: If you take this also, and any thing befall him in the way, you will bring down my grey hairs with sorrow unto hell.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos te tämän myös minulta viette pois, ja hänelle tapahtuis jotakin pahaa, niin te saattaisitte minun harmaat karvani murheella hautaan.
verses.king_james: And if ye take this also from me, and mischief befall him, ye shall bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur si intravero ad servum tuum patrem nostrum, et puer defuerit (cum anima illius ex hujus anima pendeat),
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֗ה כְּבֹאִי֙ אֶל־עַבְדְּךָ֣ אָבִ֔י וְהַנַּ֖עַר אֵינֶ֣נּוּ אִתָּ֑נוּ וְנַפְשׁ֖וֹ קְשׁוּרָ֥ה בְנַפְשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Therefore, if I shall go to thy servant, our father, and the boy be wanting, (whereas his life dependeth upon the life of him,)
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos minä nyt tulisin sinun palvelias minun isäni tykö, ja ei olisi nuorukainen meidän kanssamme: sillä hänen henkensä riippuu tämän hengestä.
verses.king_james: Now therefore when I come to thy servant my father, and the lad be not with us; seeing that his life is bound up in the lad’s life;

verses.latin_vulgate: videritque eum non esse nobiscum, morietur, et deducent famuli tui canos ejus cum dolore ad inferos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֗ה כִּרְאוֹת֛וֹ כִּי־אֵ֥ין הַנַּ֖עַר וָמֵ֑ת וְהוֹרִ֨ידוּ עֲבָדֶ֜יךָ אֶת־שֵׂיבַ֨ת עַבְדְּךָ֥ אָבִ֛ינוּ בְּיָג֖וֹן שְׁאֹֽלָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he shall see that he is not with us, he will die, and thy servants shall bring down his grey hairs with sorrow unto hell.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tapahtuis, koska hän näkis, ettei nuorukainen kanssa olisi, että hän kuolis; niin me sinun palvelias saattaisimme sinun palvelias meidän isämme harmaat karvat murheella hautaan.
verses.king_james: It shall come to pass, when he seeth that the lad is not with us, that he will die: and thy servants shall bring down the gray hairs of thy servant our father with sorrow to the grave.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego proprie servus tuus sim qui in meam hunc recepi fidem, et spopondi dicens : Nisi reduxero eum, peccati reus ero in patrem meum omni tempore.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י עַבְדְּךָ֙ עָרַ֣ב אֶת־הַנַּ֔עַר מֵעִ֥ם אָבִ֖י לֵאמֹ֑ר אִם־לֹ֤א אֲבִיאֶ֙נּוּ֙ אֵלֶ֔יךָ וְחָטָ֥אתִי לְאָבִ֖י כׇּל־הַיָּמִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let me be thy proper servant, who took him into my trust, and promised, saying: If I bring him not again, I will be guilty of sin against my father for ever.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä sinun palvelias on taannut nuorukaisen minun isäni edessä, sanoen: jollen minä häntä tuo jällensä sinun tykös, niin minä sen edestä kaiken minun elinaikani olen vikapää kärsimään.
verses.king_james: For thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father, saying, If I bring him not unto thee, then I shall bear the blame to my father for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Manebo itaque servus tuus pro puero in ministerio domini mei, et puer ascendat cum fratribus suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֗ה יֵֽשֶׁב־נָ֤א עַבְדְּךָ֙ תַּ֣חַת הַנַּ֔עַר עֶ֖בֶד לַֽאדֹנִ֑י וְהַנַּ֖עַר יַ֥עַל עִם־אֶחָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: Therefore I, thy servant, will stay instead of the boy in the service of my lord, and let the boy go up with his brethren.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja nyt siis jääkään sinun palvelias nuorukaisen edestä minun herralleni orjaksi: vaan nuorukainen menkään veljeinsä kanssa.
verses.king_james: Now therefore, I pray thee, let thy servant abide instead of the lad a bondman to my lord; and let the lad go up with his brethren.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim possum redire ad patrem meum, absente puero : ne calamitatis, quæ oppressura est patrem meum, testis assistam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־אֵיךְ֙ אֶֽעֱלֶ֣ה אֶל־אָבִ֔י וְהַנַּ֖עַר אֵינֶ֣נּוּ אִתִּ֑י פֶּ֚ן אֶרְאֶ֣ה בָרָ֔ע אֲשֶׁ֥ר יִמְצָ֖א אֶת־אָבִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For I cannot return to my father without the boy, lest I be a witness of the calamity that will oppress my father.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä kuinka minä taidan mennä minun isäni tykö, jollei nuorukainen olisi minun kanssani? Etten minä näkisi sitä surkeutta, johon minun isäni joutuu.
verses.king_james: For how shall I go up to my father, and the lad be not with me? lest peradventure I see the evil that shall come on my father.
